Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Tessa Moreno" or "BrightLoop" or similar) to find the original status-update email from Tessa Moreno to Mina Hashimoto asking about her three employees' relocations, and Julian Brooks's forward of that email to Zara Kovacevic delegating the investigation. Also find related BrightLoop relocation correspondence including Simone Richter's apartment complaint, Marcus Webb's support messages, and the stipend audit thread.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or similar) to find Simone's April 8 complaint that the UrbanNest apartment was a studio instead of the promised one-bedroom. Also find Mina Hashimoto's April 9 reply promising an end-of-week update that was never sent, Julian Brooks's later apology email to Simone, and Mina's internal escalation to Chloe Vance and David Chen documenting the unresolved complaint.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" or similar) to find Road Runner Auto Transport's April 11 delay notice to Blessing Okafor saying the 2019 Honda Civic was stuck at the Indianapolis transfer hub due to a driver shortage, with a revised ETA of April 18 to 20 versus the original April 8 delivery. Also find Marcus's two follow-up emails to support@moveops.com asking for an ETA (the second CC'd to Tessa Moreno), Blessing's admission that she never acted on the delay notice, and Julian Brooks's eventual direct reply to Marcus apologizing and promising a same-day update.
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Jordan Ekwueme" or "stipend" or "TechComfort" or similar) to find Dmitri Volkov's fraud audit email flagging Jordan's $45 monitor stand receipt from TechComfort Supply (STIP-2026-0097) due to PDF template metadata matching receipts from other clients. Also find Marcus Thorne's confirmation that the $45 is on administrative hold and has not been disbursed, and Emeka Diallo's outreach email to Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop requesting verification of the purchase.
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "BrightLoop" or "Simone Richter" or "Marcus Webb" or similar) to find internal discussions about the operational gaps. Expected findings include Chloe Vance's post in #operations about the unactioned Road Runner delay and the studio versus one-bedroom mismatch, Mina Hashimoto's BrightLoop failure reconstruction, Julian's plan to handle both employee responses directly, and team discussions about coordinator capacity.
Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop Analytics" or similar) to find the company record (company_brightloop, AM: Mina Hashimoto, HR contact: Tessa Moreno). Search deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_brightloop" or dealname: "BrightLoop" or similar) to find the April 2026 relocations deal for Simone Richter and Marcus Webb ($11,350, closedwon) and the Q2 2026 deal for Jordan Ekwueme ($7,000, qualifiedtobuy). List engagements using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_brightloop"]) to check existing engagement history on the account.
Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Company", value: "BrightLoop Analytics") or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) to find statuses for Simone Richter (Chicago to Boston, In Progress, April 4 to 6, Coordinator: Suki Patel, rush five-day turnaround), Marcus Webb (Atlanta to Boston, In Progress, April 14 to 18, Coordinator: Suki Patel, vehicle shipping via Road Runner for 2019 Honda Civic), and Jordan Ekwueme (San Diego to Boston, Completed, April 25 to 26, Coordinator: Suki Patel).
Search Airtable Stipend Transactions using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Stipend Transactions, field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Jordan Ekwueme") or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Stipend Transactions) to find the flagged $45 monitor stand submission (TechComfort StandPro Mini, Approval Status: Pending Approval, Approved Amount: $0, audit flag for PDF Template Match using the same TechComfort Supply Invoice Generator v2.1 template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041 seen in the Kevin Tran and Andre Washington receipts) and the approved $299.99 WiFi system which had no issues.
Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or similar) to find existing issues, including the BrightLoop account audit issue assigned to Mina Hashimoto (Priority 1, titled "BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion") and the ops gaps documentation issue assigned to Chloe Vance (Priority 2, titled "Document BrightLoop ops gaps: Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace, Priya ADA handling, Oliver UK workflow"). Read comments using linear_list_comments on each issue to find Julian Brooks's notes about service recovery actions and Blessing Okafor's explanation of the root cause of the Marcus Webb miss.
Look up Julian Brooks's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Julian Brooks" or similar). Find julian.brooks@moveops.com. Also look up Zara Kovacevic's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Zara Kovacevic" or similar). Find zara.kovacevic@moveops.com.
Log an engagement on the BrightLoop Analytics account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: compiled findings, company_ids: ["company_brightloop"]) documenting Tessa Moreno's status inquiry and the findings for all three employees: Simone Richter's apartment mismatch (studio instead of one-bedroom, missed follow-up, Julian now handling), Marcus Webb's vehicle delay (Road Runner April 11 notice went unactioned, two unanswered support emails, Julian now responding), and Jordan Ekwueme's stipend hold ($45 monitor stand flagged in fraud audit, Emeka contacted Tessa for verification, still pending).
Send an email using send_email (sender: "zara.kovacevic@moveops.com", recipients: ["julian.brooks@moveops.com"]) with the compiled status for all three BrightLoop employees. Cover Simone Richter's apartment issue (placed in a studio instead of the promised one-bedroom by UrbanNest, Mina's April 9 follow-up was missed, Julian has since stepped in), Marcus Webb's vehicle delay (Road Runner delay notice April 11, Honda Civic stuck at Indianapolis hub, revised ETA April 18 to 20, two unanswered support emails from Marcus, Julian has since replied), and Jordan Ekwueme's stipend hold ($45 TechComfort Supply monitor stand flagged in fraud audit due to PDF template match, on administrative hold pending BrightLoop verification via Emeka's outreach to Tessa).
Post a message using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C007", payload: summary message) in the #customer-support channel with a heads-up that Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop Analytics has reached out asking for a status update on her employees' relocations. Summarize the three open items (Simone Richter apartment mismatch, Marcus Webb vehicle delay, Jordan Ekwueme stipend hold), note that Julian is already aware and working on a response to Tessa, and flag that no one else should reply to Tessa separately without knowing the full context first.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
